definitions.text: To revolve by turning over and over; to move by turning on a horizontal axis; to impel forward with a revolving motion on a supporting surface.
examples: To roll a wheel, a ball, or a barrel.
The child will roll on the floor.

definitions.text: To wrap (something) round on itself; to form into a spherical or cylindrical body by causing to turn over and over.
examples: To roll a sheet of paper; to roll clay or putty into a ball.
The cloth rolls unevenly; the snow rolls well.

definitions.text: To bind or involve by winding, as with a bandage; to enwrap; often with up.
examples: To roll up the vase in bubble wrap.

definitions.text: The act or result of rolling, or state of being rolled.
examples: Look at the roll of the waves.

definitions.text: Something formed into a compact shape by being rolled up.

examples: a roll of fat, of wool, paper, cloth, etc.

definitions.text: A document written on a piece of parchment, paper, or other materials which may be rolled up; a scroll.
An official or public document; a register; a record.
